errors.title: Something went wrong

settings.title: Settings
settings.appDescription: Assets Tracker is designed to help you visualize your high-level wealth journey. By tracking asset growth through periodic snapshots, you can focus on the big picture instead of daily trading noise.
settings.syncPricesDesc: Fetch the latest market prices from Yahoo Finance and CoinGecko.
settings.syncSnapshotDesc: Manually record your current balances to the history chart.
settings.baseCurrencyDescription: Used for portfolio totals and converted values
settings.currencySearchDescription: Search by currency code, name, or symbol.
settings.preferencesSaved: Currency and language are saved. Display density and color apply immediately.
settings.preferencesUnsaved: Save to apply currency or language changes.
settings.densityDescription: Control the spacing of cards and rows
settings.themeDescription: Choose light, dark, or follow your system preference
settings.colorSchemaDescription: Choose an accent color for light and dark mode
settings.syncMarketDataDesc: Update asset prices and exchange rates together.

privacy.title: Privacy & Security
privacy.summaryDescription: Keep sensitive values private when you are in public, export a backup when you need one, and sign out from this device.
privacy.privacyModeDescription: Hide balances and account values across the app.
privacy.sessionDescription: Your deployment controls authentication and session security.
privacy.sessionDescriptionWithEmail: Signed in as {email}. Your deployment controls authentication and session security.
privacy.exportBackupDescription: Download your accounts, holdings, and transaction history as JSON.
privacy.yourDataDescription: Assets Tracker does not connect to your bank or brokerage accounts. Financial records are the entries you add here.

settings.signOutDesc: Securely log out of your account on this device.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.emptyDescription: Start by adding your first account — a bank account, brokerage, crypto wallet, or anything else you want to track.
dashboard.emptyHint: Snapshots are captured daily, so charts fill in as your history grows.

onboarding.title: Your dashboard is ready for the first account.
onboarding.description: Add one account to start calculating net worth, allocation, currency exposure, and history from the same source of truth.

preview.chartLabel: Preview of the net worth trend chart before account data is added

account.title: Add one account
account.description: Start with a bank, brokerage, wallet, property, or liability.

currency.title: Confirm currency
currency.description: Choose the base currency used for totals and converted values.

history.title: Build history
history.description: Daily snapshots fill charts after your first tracked balance.

aside.title: What unlocks next
aside.description: Once an account exists, this view becomes your live cockpit for changes, allocation, exposure, goals, and performance.
aside.progressHint: The first account unlocks dashboard charts immediately.

goals.title: Goals
goals.subtitle: Track milestones toward your net worth targets.
goals.manageOrderHint: Drag to reorder your goals. New goals are added to the bottom.
goals.emptyDescription: Set a target — like "$1M net worth by 2030" — and track your progress here.
goals.emptyHint: Establish goals to track your milestones over time.

onboarding.title: Turn a number into a trackable milestone.
onboarding.description: Create a goal with a target amount, scope, and optional date so progress can stay visible while balances change.

preview.cardSubtitle: Progress appears after the goal has a target and a matching source.

goal.title: Set target
goal.description: Name the milestone and enter the amount.

scope.title: Choose scope
scope.description: Track net worth, assets, a category, or one account.

deadline.title: Add date
deadline.description: Use a deadline when timing matters.

aside.title: What goals add
aside.description: Goals give the dashboard a planning rail and make projections easier to interpret.
aside.progressHint: A goal can use existing accounts immediately, or wait for accounts added later.

form.name: Goal Name
form.namePlaceholder: e.g. $1M Net Worth, House Down Payment
form.targetDateHint: Optional — leave blank for an open-ended goal.

goalsMilestone.title: Goals
goalsMilestone.projectionHint: Estimate when you'll reach financial independence.

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.subtitle: Calculated from saved snapshots and cash-flow records.
analysis.assetsVsLiabilitiesSubtitle: Assets and liabilities use each month's latest snapshot.
analysis.portfolioReturnMethodology: Portfolio Return covers brokerage and crypto accounts only; deposits count at half weight (Modified Dietz approximation). Not annualized.
analysis.analysisSummarySubtitle: Year-to-date is always shown; other metrics follow {range}.
analysis.kpiMethodology: YTD compares the latest snapshot with the year-start baseline. Range rows use month-end net-worth changes inside {range}.
analysis.movementSectionSubtitle: How net worth changed, and how cash flow explains it.
analysis.compositionSectionSubtitle: Where value sits, and which accounts drove the selected range.
analysis.noData: Not enough snapshots yet. Analysis appears after a few days of history.
analysis.emptyHint: Snapshots are captured daily, so charts fill in as your history grows.
analysis.emptyTitle: Track how your net worth changes
analysis.emptyBody: Once daily snapshots accrue, this tab shows month-end assets, net-worth change, cash flow, and account attribution.

onboarding.titleNoAccounts: Build the base for real analysis
onboarding.descriptionNoAccounts: Add the accounts you want tracked, then snapshots turn them into movement, cash flow, and attribution views.
onboarding.descriptionWithAccounts: Your accounts are ready. Once snapshots accumulate, this page becomes a compact read on movement, composition, and account contribution.

preview.trendSubtitle: Month-end snapshots become the analysis spine.

accounts.title: Add accounts
accounts.description: Choose the balances and holdings that should feed the cockpit.

snapshots.title: Capture snapshots
snapshots.description: Daily records create the month-end baselines for every chart.

read.title: Read movement
read.description: Compare cash flow, market movement, and account-level attribution.

aside.title: Analysis needs a trail
aside.description: This tab stays quiet until there is enough history to compare beginning, ending, and monthly values.
aside.progressHintNoAccounts: Start with accounts; the system handles the snapshot rhythm after that.
aside.progressHintWithAccounts: Accounts are connected. Let the first snapshots land, then return for movement and composition.

analysis.heatmapSubtitle: Accounts sized by current asset value. Select an account to inspect holdings.
analysis.heatmapEmpty: No positive asset accounts to show in composition yet.
analysis.heatmapUnpricedNote: {count, plural, one {Composition excludes # holding without a cached price.} other {Composition excludes # holdings without cached prices.}}
analysis.heatmapPrivacyDescription: Allocation and holdings are hidden while privacy mode is on.
analysis.heatmapChartLabel: Portfolio composition for {count, plural, one {# asset account} other {# asset accounts}}. Select an account to inspect holdings.
analysis.cashFlowSubtitle: Monthly net-worth change split into cash flow and residual movement.
analysis.categoryTrendSubtitle: Category totals use each month's latest snapshot.
analysis.categoryTrendSubtitleLimited: Showing top {count} of {total} categories by latest value.
analysis.cumulativeGrowthSubtitle: Running total of deposits vs. market gains over the range.
analysis.cumulativeGrowthNote: Market is cumulative net-worth change minus net deposits and withdrawals.
analysis.drawdownSubtitle: How far net worth sits below its prior peak.
analysis.drawdownNote: 0% means at an all-time high; dips show peak-to-trough declines.
analysis.investmentCostBasisSubtitle: Current investment market value compared with remaining cost basis.
analysis.costBasisPartialNote: Cost basis and unrealized gain cover only holdings with buy unit prices.
analysis.returnTrendSubtitle: Monthly investment return and cumulative index, brokerage and crypto accounts only.
analysis.returnTrendNote: Monthly returns use the Modified Dietz approximation (deposits at half weight); the line chains them geometrically. Not annualized.
analysis.cashFlowNote: Market performance is monthly change minus net deposits and withdrawals.
analysis.attributionSubtitle: Account change over the selected range, split by cash flow and market movement.
analysis.attributionNoData: Not enough history to compute attribution.
analysis.attributionNote: Market = ending value minus starting value minus account cash flow.

projections.title: Projections
projections.subtitle: Estimate when you'll reach financial independence.
projections.emptyHint: Projections are estimates based on your current savings and performance.

onboarding.titleNoAccounts: Give the projection engine a portfolio
onboarding.descriptionNoAccounts: Add accounts first so the model has a current net worth, savings trail, and a real portfolio to project from.
onboarding.descriptionWithAccounts: Your accounts are ready. Once snapshots exist, this page can estimate milestones, FIRE timing, and assumption sensitivity.

preview.chartSubtitle: Historical net worth anchors the projected curve.

accounts.title: Add accounts
accounts.description: Use active balances as the starting point for the model.

history.title: Build history
history.description: Snapshots give the chart real anchors and savings context.

assumptions.title: Tune inputs
assumptions.description: Adjust expenses, savings, return, inflation, and withdrawal rate.

aside.title: A projection starts with evidence
aside.description: The forecast stays useful when it is grounded in recorded net worth instead of a blank estimate.
aside.progressHintNoAccounts: Add the portfolio first; history and assumptions will have something real to work with.
aside.progressHintWithAccounts: Accounts are connected. Capture snapshots to unlock the model.

projections.withdrawalRateHint: The % of your portfolio you spend each year in retirement. At 4% (the classic rule), your FIRE Number is 25× your annual expenses — historically enough to last 30+ years.
projections.noData: Take a net worth snapshot to start projecting.
projections.step1Desc: Enter your Annual Expenses to calculate your FIRE Number — the portfolio value that can fund your lifestyle indefinitely without working.
projections.step2Desc: Annual Savings is pre-filled from your last 12 months of deposits. Adjust the expected return and withdrawal rate to match your situation.
projections.step3Desc: The chart overlays your actual net worth history (solid line) with the projected path (dashed). Markers show where your portfolio crosses the FIRE target.
projections.fireRuleBody: The withdrawal rate is not your yearly expenses — it's the percentage you draw from your total portfolio each year. At 4%, your portfolio needs to be large enough that 4% of it equals your annual spending:
projections.fireRuleFootnote: The remaining portfolio stays invested and keeps growing, which is why 4% has historically lasted 30+ years. A lower rate (e.g. 3%) requires a larger portfolio but is safer; a higher rate (e.g. 5%) needs less but carries more risk.
projections.indFireNumberDesc: The total portfolio size you need to retire. Calculated as Annual Expenses ÷ Withdrawal Rate. At 4%, this equals 25× your annual spending.
projections.indCurrentNWDesc: Your most recent tracked net worth from the latest daily snapshot. This is the starting point for all projections.
projections.indProgressDesc: Current Net Worth as a percentage of your FIRE Number. Reaches 100% the moment you're financially independent.
projections.indFireDateDesc: The estimated year your portfolio crosses the FIRE Number, assuming your inputs hold constant. Based on deterministic (not Monte Carlo) math.
projections.inflationRateHint: Restates future value in today's purchasing power.
projections.resultNoExpenses: Add your annual expenses to project a FIRE date
projections.resultNoExpensesHint: Until then, here is where your savings trajectory leads.
projections.milestonesSubtitle: When your portfolio crosses each mark on the current plan.
projections.lensHintNominal: Future values shown in that year's dollars.
projections.lensHintReal: Future values shown in today's purchasing power.

login.title: Assets Tracker
login.subtitle: Securely access your financial portfolio
login.trust1: Authentication stays under your deployment's control
login.trust2: Your data is private & never sold
login.trust3: Only you can see your portfolio
login.footerBefore: Your data stays private. Read our

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How Assets Tracker handles your data
privacy.section1Body: Depending on your deployment, you can sign in with Google or a self-host password. Google sign-in provides your name, email address, and profile picture. Any financial data (accounts, holdings, balances) is entered by you manually and stored in your private database.
privacy.section2Body: We never access your real bank or brokerage accounts. We do not collect your passwords, browsing history, location, or contacts.
privacy.section3Body: We fetch public market prices from Yahoo Finance and CoinGecko using only the ticker symbols you enter. No personal information is sent to these services.
privacy.section4Body: Your data is never sold, rented, or shared with third parties for any purpose.
privacy.section5Body: You can export all your data or delete your account at any time from the Settings page.
privacy.section6Body: Authentication is configured by the deployment owner. Google OAuth never shares your Google password with the app; self-host passwords remain in the deployment environment and are not stored in the application database.
privacy.section7Body: We use Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights to understand basic usage and page performance. These tools do not include your manually entered account names, holdings, balances, or transaction notes.
privacy.section8Body: For privacy or data requests, email us at

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: Rules and guidelines for using Assets Tracker
terms.section1Body: By accessing and using Assets Tracker, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provision of this agreement. If you do not agree to abide by these terms, please do not use this service.
terms.section2Body: Assets Tracker is provided 'as is' for personal financial tracking. You agree to use the service only for lawful purposes and in a way that does not infringe the rights of, restrict or inhibit anyone else's use and enjoyment of the service.
terms.section3Body: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account and for all activities that occur under your account. We reserve the right to refuse service, terminate accounts, or remove or edit content in our sole discretion.
terms.section4Body: The information provided by Assets Tracker is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or usefulness of any market data provided.
terms.section5Body: Assets Tracker shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential or exemplary damages resulting from your use of the service or any reliance on the information provided.
terms.section6Body: We reserve the right to modify these terms from time to time at our sole discretion. Your continued use of the service following the posting of changes to these terms will mean you accept those changes.
